items.subject: Follow-up: Gaia-Lumen preliminary review request
items.body:
Dear Dr. Vakoch,

I am following up on the Gaia-Lumen preliminary review request sent on 2026-06-14.

The project has since been consolidated into a shorter technical package that clearly separates confirmed astronomical data, derived values, model parameters and speculative elements. It does not claim discovery of a new planet and does not request any unauthorized transmission.

Could you advise whether this package is appropriate for METI review, routing, archival guidance or whether there is a more suitable scientific contact?

Main documents:

- GAIA_LUMEN_PUBLIC_BRIEF.html
- GAIA_LUMEN_DOSSIER_SCIENTIFICO_BREVE.pdf
- GAIA_LUMEN_BIBLIOGRAFIA_PARAMETRI.md

Thank you for any guidance you can provide.

Sincerely,

Gaia-Lumen Project

items.subject: Follow-up: Gaia-Lumen preliminary review and routing request
items.body:
Dear SETI Institute team,

I am following up on the Gaia-Lumen routing request sent on 2026-06-14.

The project has been consolidated into a concise technical package that distinguishes confirmed astronomical data, derived values, model parameters and speculative elements. It does not claim discovery of a new planet and does not request any unauthorized transmission.

Could you advise whether there is an appropriate SETI, astrobiology or technosignature-related review channel for this material, or whether it should be redirected elsewhere?

Main documents:

- GAIA_LUMEN_PUBLIC_BRIEF.html
- GAIA_LUMEN_DOSSIER_SCIENTIFICO_BREVE.pdf
- GAIA_LUMEN_BIBLIOGRAFIA_PARAMETRI.md

Thank you for any guidance you can provide.

Sincerely,

Gaia-Lumen Project

items.subject: Epsilon Eridani parameter check for a modeling project
items.body:
Dear Exoplanet Archive team,

I am preparing a technical modeling dossier that uses Epsilon Eridani as a reference star. The project does not claim any new planet discovery.

Could you please advise whether the following cited parameters are correctly represented or whether I should adjust the wording/source citation?

- Epsilon Eridani / HD 22049 / HIP 16537 / Gliese 144
- approximate distance: 10.475 light years
- stellar type: K2V
- confirmed planet in the system: Epsilon Eridani b / AEgir

The only purpose of the request is to avoid misquoting public exoplanet data.

Thank you.
